purpose: Durable P16 aiflow queue for post-P15 Gateway and Cache benchmark convergence, keeping P15 completion evidence machine-checkable while opening the next focused governance batch.

aiflowSubmission.runtimeStatePolicy: .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, bench/current.txt, bench/regression-report.json, bench/summary.md, bin/gofly, and docs/superpowers remain ignored local evidence and must not be committed.
aiflowSubmission.safetyPolicy: P16 tasks are submitted to ignored local aiflow runtime state and mirrored here as durable docs/reference evidence; each task must be completed in order and committed by the current agent or human only after its gate passes or an environment-limited downgrade is documented.

previousBatchHandoff.completionPolicy: P15 is treated as completed only when all three task rows in docs/reference/governance-p15-roadmap.json carry completion status, verification commands, and pending-current-commit placeholders owned by their per-task commits.
previousBatchHandoff.runtimeStatePolicy: Local .aiflow queue data is runtime evidence only; durable completion evidence belongs in docs/reference/governance-p15-roadmap.json and docs/reference/governance-boundary-inventory.json.

tasks.title: P15 completion handoff
tasks.objective: Convert P15 from active completed roadmap evidence into the latest completed governance batch and open P16 as the next active aiflow batch.
tasks.deliverable: P15 completed task evidence, P16 active roadmap, boundary inventory transition, dashboard convergence fields, and aiflow runtime-state exclusion proof.
tasks.commitPolicy: commit after the P15 completion handoff and P16 active-batch evidence pass boundary inventory and governance report gates without staging ignored runtime state

tasks.title: Gateway cache trend sample attachment
tasks.objective: Attach durable Gateway and Cache five-sample baseline, three-sample current trend, cache-mode notes, and rollback evidence without promoting weak latency claims.
tasks.deliverable: Gateway/cache sample attachment policy, benchmark trend evidence inventory, cache-mode notes, allocation-only candidate boundary, and rollback evidence.
tasks.commitPolicy: commit after Gateway/Cache trend sample evidence passes bench-regression-check and remains outside trackedBenchmarks unless every promotion prerequisite is present

tasks.title: Gateway cache allocation promotion review
tasks.objective: Review whether exactly one Gateway or Cache surface can enter allocation-blocking coverage, or keep promotion held with narrowed blockers.
tasks.deliverable: Promotion decision for one selected Gateway/Cache surface or an explicit hold decision with blocker diff, release note policy, and rollback guardrails.
tasks.commitPolicy: commit after allocation promotion review passes bench-regression-check and keeps latency report-only unless maxRegressionRatio and rollback evidence are durable
